title: Business Breakfast | Stock Market News | June 19, 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: NSE stake ~Rs 5,400 cr (=10% of LIC mkt cap); signs of +10% once NSE issue factors into NAV.
recommendations.detail: Speculative-longs / watch list. NSE stake value alone is ~Rs 5,400 cr, which is 10% of LIC's market cap; once the NSE issue happens that gets factored into NAV, so LIC has signs of rising 10%. Stock may go up to 460 or 470 in the near-to-medium term; don't ignore it.

recommendations.note: Multibagger / 2-3 bagger potential; capacity expansion, electric trucks, BYD battery tie-up, old-bus replacement.
recommendations.detail: Sees more than 15-20%/yr - multibagger returns; from here a likely 2-3 bagger. Capacity expansion underway, entering electric trucks, BYD battery tie-up, and India's hard line on China plus old-bus/vehicle replacement (Delhi, Mumbai) are tailwinds. Picked it first from ~90.

recommendations.note: Not a great/wealth-creating stock; rises seasonally then falls. Hold.
recommendations.detail: I'd say hold; recently activity in the stock has increased. But these aren't great stocks to buy and not wealth-creating - they rise seasonally when the market is good and then fall.

recommendations.note: Only for positional trading; IOC/BPCL/HPCL are better bets for the future.
recommendations.detail: Only for positional trading; the stock moves very swiftly (1,000+ to ~750 then back above 1,100). Parent-controlled with fixed margins so the valuation holds, but if you ask me IOC, BPCL or HPCL would be better bets in the future.

recommendations.note: Don't buy large quantity now; range-bound, underperforming. Wait for June-end results.
recommendations.detail: In such large quantity, not immediately. The company is stabilising slowly but not showing expected performance, so the stock is range-bound; it has held above 200 for many days. I would wait for the June-end result before deciding.

recommendations.note: On-and-off performer; dividend play. Don't enter with great hopes.
recommendations.detail: ITC and IOC perform on-and-off and can lie flat for a couple of years. In IOC there's a dividend play, especially as crude prices keep moving back and forth; my advice is don't step in with great hopes on these.

recommendations.note: Speculative but fundamentally fine; Rs 10 paid-up, operator-driven. Hold; buying fresh is a gamble.
recommendations.detail: Fundamentally looks fine; put it as a slightly speculative bet. Rode the recent CCL rally to 180 then corrected to ~150; being a Rs 10 paid-up stock some activity continues, so hold for some time. Buying fresh is at the operator's mercy - it's in his fist.
